text.title: Audience-Driven Pivot Decision Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Audience-Driven Pivot Decision application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Confirm audience is healthy (open rate >25%, engagement steady or growing) BEFORE pivoting product. If audience is sick, product is not the only problem.
rule.rationale: Pivoting product on top of decaying audience compounds problems; audience health is a precondition.

rule.statement: Brainstorm 3-5 adjacent product candidates the same audience would plausibly buy. Each candidate has a 1-sentence value prop + price band.
rule.rationale: Single-option pivots underperform; 3-5 forces comparative judgment.

rule.statement: Run a paid validation test (presale, paid LOI, pre-order page) for the top candidate. ≥10 paying signals before committing to build.
rule.rationale: Free interest is noise; only payment validates demand.

rule.statement: Announce sunset of old product with ≥30-day notice + refund policy + data export. Audience trust > short-term revenue.
rule.rationale: Audience-first means audience trust is the asset; mistreating exiting customers destroys it.

rule.statement: After 90 days post-pivot, evaluate: new MRR > old MRR baseline? If not, return to candidate list, do not iterate the new product blindly.
rule.rationale: Anti-sunk-cost: 90 days is enough to see direction; honest re-evaluation prevents 2-year zombie products.
